preset_cases.hook: Average response looks promising until the SK-MEL-5 subclone survives the first BRAF-heavy picks.

steps.why_chosen: Strong expected mean response plus early chance to expose a BRAF-resistant pocket.
Follow-up MEK signal tests whether the resistant clone is pathway-limited or still exposed downstream.
Broader kinase coverage checks whether robustness matters more than peak average effect.
The model still sees uncertainty around the resistant clone, so it probes outside the MAPK path.
Confirms the broader read: downstream pathway inhibition is more robust than the original BRAF hit.
Low-confidence off-pathway probe to close the loop before final recommendation.

final.main_realization: Vemurafenib looked strongest at first glance, but SK-MEL-5 persisted. MEK inhibition held up better across all three subclones.
final.next_experiment: Test Trametinib next as the more robust heterogeneity-resistant option.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner exposed the resistant subclone by test 1 and reached a robust recommendation by test 2. Random never separated average effect from resistance risk within 6 tests.

steps.why_chosen: Random baseline pick.
Random baseline eventually hits the obvious average winner.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline finally finds the more robust option late.
Random baseline pick.

final.main_realization: Random found the same robust drug eventually, but only after spending most of the budget on weaker probes.
final.next_experiment: Use Trametinib if you only get one validation test, but the discovery path was inefficient.
final.active_vs_random: Random required 5 tests to reach the same recommendation the active learner had by test 2.

preset_cases.hook: A small responder clone is hidden under two slower, noisier populations.

steps.why_chosen: Tests whether the hidden responder signal sits in a PI3K-dependent luminal subclone.
Narrows the responder hypothesis and separates hormone-driven sensitivity from the triple-negative tail.
After two endocrine-heavy hits, the model checks whether the small aggressive clone is the stronger actionable responder.
Low-cost uncertainty reduction around the luminal branch.
Rules out a stronger DNA-repair-specific opportunity.
Final check on pathway-adjacent luminal sensitivity.

final.main_realization: The luminal clones responded to endocrine-pathway drugs, but the strongest hidden responder was the smaller MDA-MB-231-like subclone under Paclitaxel.
final.next_experiment: Validate Paclitaxel if the goal is to expose the hidden responder rather than optimize the average luminal signal.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner discovered the minority responder by test 3. Random never isolated that story cleanly.

steps.why_chosen: Random baseline pick.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline hits a luminal responder but still misses the hidden aggressive clone story.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline reaches the hidden responder at the end of the budget.

final.main_realization: Random still found the responder, but only after using the full budget on mixed signals.
final.next_experiment: Paclitaxel remains the right validation if the objective is to expose the small aggressive responder clone.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner found the hidden responder three tests earlier than random.

preset_cases.hook: No single clone dominates the average, so the safest drug is the one that degrades all of them moderately well.

steps.why_chosen: Good first-pass average and low risk of one clone escaping completely.
Checks if the robust choice can be improved by a stronger but less uniform pathway hit.
Once MEK proved uneven, the learner shifted to the most consistent cross-clone option.
Sanity check on an EGFR-biased alternative before recommending chemotherapy robustness.
Confirms the platform’s robust-drug ranking among standard colon options.
Final broad-spectrum check.

final.main_realization: The flashier pathway-specific picks left one clone behind. Oxaliplatin was the first compound that suppressed all three consistently.
final.next_experiment: Validate Oxaliplatin if robustness across heterogeneity matters more than chasing a single standout subclone hit.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner found the robust drug by test 3. Random drifted across uneven mechanisms longer.

steps.why_chosen: Random baseline pick.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline improves, but still not the most uniform option.
Random baseline reaches a strong average late.
Random baseline finally lands on the robust option at the end.

final.main_realization: Random needed the full budget to discover the same robust choice.
final.next_experiment: Oxaliplatin is still the right follow-up, but the search path wasted comparison capacity.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner reached the robust choice three tests sooner.

preset_cases.hook: A kinase-heavy average response hides a second lung clone that stays flat until the agent tests outside EGFR signaling.

steps.why_chosen: High expected lung-pathway signal, but the two-clone split makes resistance easy to expose early.
After the EGFR escape signal, the agent probes a downstream route predicted to hit both clones.
Checks whether the resistant clone is better explained by an alternate kinase dependency.
Broad cytotoxic benchmark to see if robustness beats pathway specificity.
Confirms that receptor-level targeting remains vulnerable to the same resistant clone.
Final off-pathway probe before recommending the more uniform MEK result.

final.main_realization: EGFR inhibition created a clean average signal but left NCI-H460 resistant. MEK inhibition was less flashy and more uniform.
final.next_experiment: Test Cobimetinib next because it suppressed both lung subclones instead of only the EGFR-sensitive one.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner exposed receptor-level resistance by test 1 and found the robust downstream option by test 2.

steps.why_chosen: Random baseline pick.
Random baseline sees resistance but cannot prioritize the follow-up.
Random baseline improves through a broad cytotoxic.
Random baseline confirms the same receptor-level escape late.
Random baseline pick.
Random baseline finds the robust option only at the end.

final.main_realization: Random eventually found the same robust drug, but only after spending most of the budget on uneven probes.
final.next_experiment: Cobimetinib remains the validation choice, but the path was inefficient.
final.active_vs_random: Random reached the robust option four tests later than the active learner.

preset_cases.hook: A fast two-clone scenario for live demos where the question is whether a broad VEGF-pathway drug is more reliable than a single sharp hit.

steps.why_chosen: High expected renal relevance and low predicted spread across the two clones.
Tests whether a broader kinase profile improves the smaller A498 clone without losing the dominant clone.
Compares a narrower VEGFR-like profile against the first robust hit.
Off-pathway check with low confidence, useful as a negative comparator.
Broad stress response probe before committing to the VEGF-pathway recommendation.
Final related kinase benchmark; useful, but not as strong as the first two hits.

final.main_realization: The simplest renal-focused pick was also the most balanced across both clones. Broader kinase probes were close but did not improve the mixed response.
final.next_experiment: Validate Pazopanib first as the robust two-clone option, with Cabozantinib as the immediate backup.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner reached the robust answer on test 1; random needed most of the episode to get there.

steps.why_chosen: Random baseline pick.
Random baseline improves but stays nonspecific.
Random baseline reaches a useful but not best kinase option.
Random baseline finds a stronger renal signal late.
Random baseline finally reaches the most balanced hit.
Random baseline confirms the backup after already spending the budget.

final.main_realization: Random reached the same answer, but only after several off-target or weaker probes.
final.next_experiment: Pazopanib is still the next validation, with Cabozantinib as a backup.
final.active_vs_random: Active learner found the robust drug four tests sooner than random.
